title: Q1 2026 Employee Data Handling Guidelines – Compliance Note
body:
**Purpose & Scope**
This compliance note outlines data handling practices for all TechCorp personnel involved in processing, storing, or accessing employee data and personal information. These guidelines apply across all systems, applications, and workflows and reflect our commitment to GDPR and SOC 2 compliance. Adherence to these standards is mandatory and reinforces TechCorp's accountability framework.

**GDPR Compliance – Core Principles**
TechCorp operates across jurisdictions with EU personnel and therefore must comply with GDPR requirements for employee data. Specific obligations include: (1) Transparency and Fairness: Employees must be informed in clear, accessible notices of what personal data we collect, why we collect it, how we use it, and for how long we retain it. These notices are maintained in our privacy documentation and must be updated whenever processing activities change. (2) Legal Basis: We process employee data only under documented legal bases such as legitimate business interest (payroll, performance management), legal obligation (tax records, statutory compliance), or explicit consent where applicable. Team leads should confirm the legal basis for any new processing activity with the Legal team. (3) Data Subject Rights: Employees have rights to access, correct, delete, and port their personal data. Any requests for subject access or erasure must be escalated immediately to the Legal team with full context. Response timelines are strictly 30 days. (4) Data Protection by Design: When designing systems or processes that handle employee data, security and privacy must be built in from inception—not added as an afterthought. This includes encryption at rest and in transit, access controls, and audit logging.

**SOC 2 Compliance – Data Security & Access Control**
TechCorp's SOC 2 framework governs how we secure, access, and manage all data, including employee records. Key operational requirements for engineers and team members: (1) Access Restric
